title: aeonium domesticum
intro: Aeonium domesticum, also known as the tree houseleek or nine banded succulent, is a succulent plant native to the Canary Islands. The plant's leaves contain saponins, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential source for medicinal applications. Aeonium domesticum has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of the plant have been made into infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and other health issues.
